name: Castillo de Nogales a Castillo de Salvatierra — circular desde Torre de Miguel Sesmero
description: This moderate 28.3-mile (45.5 km) mountain biking route from Torre de Miguel Sesmero connects Nogales Castle and Salvatierra Castle.
region_intro: Mountain bike trails Torre De Miguel Sesmero are set within a diverse landscape of plains, dehesas, and wooded pastures in the province of Badajoz, Extremadura. The region features extensive oak and cork oak forests, providing varied terrain for cycling. Routes often integrate natural beauty with historical landmarks, including medieval castles and ancient olive groves. This combination offers mountain bikers a mix of open stretches and shaded paths. Best mountain bike trails around Torre De Miguel Sesmero The most popular mountain bike trail is Castillo de Nogales a Castillo de Salvatierra — circular desde Torre de Miguel Sesmero, a 28.3 miles (45.5 km) trail that takes 3 hours 47 minutes to complete, connecting two historic castles. Another top favourite among local mountain bikers is Torre de Miguel Sesmero a Salvaleón — circular por el Castillo de Nogales, a moderate 22.0 miles (35.4 km) path offering varied terrain and scenic views around Nogales Castle. Local mountain bikers also love the Route of the Twelve Apostles Loop, a 18.0 miles (29.0 km) trail leading through ancient cork oak forests, often completed in about 2 hours 44 minutes. Mountain biking around Torre De Miguel Sesmero is defined by dehesa landscapes, oak and cork oak forests, and historical sites. The network offers options for easy, moderate, and difficult routes. The routes in Torre De Miguel Sesmero are highly rated by the komoot community with an average score of 5.0 stars from a handful of reviews. More than 55 mountain bikers have used komoot to explore Torre De Miguel Sesmero's varied terrain.
region_faq:
Q: How many mountain bike trails are available around Torre de Miguel Sesmero?
A: There are over 25 mountain bike trails around Torre de Miguel Sesmero, offering a variety of experiences. The network includes routes suitable for different skill levels, with the majority being of moderate difficulty.

Q: What types of landscapes can I expect on the mountain bike trails?
A: The trails in Torre de Miguel Sesmero traverse diverse landscapes, including open plains, traditional dehesas with extensive oak and cork oak forests, and wooded pastures. You'll find a mix of shaded paths and open stretches, often integrating natural beauty with historical landmarks.

Q: Are there any easy mountain bike trails suitable for beginners or families?
A: Yes, while most routes are moderate, there are a couple of easier mountain bike trails available. These routes are generally shorter and have less elevation gain, making them suitable for beginners or families looking for a more relaxed ride. You can find 2 easy routes in the area.

Q: Can I find circular mountain bike routes in Torre de Miguel Sesmero?
A: Absolutely. Many of the mountain bike trails around Torre de Miguel Sesmero are circular, allowing you to start and finish in the same location. For example, the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e1359071536/castillo-de-nogales-a-castillo-de-salvatierra-circular-desde-torre-de-miguel-sesmero" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Castillo de Nogales a Castillo de Salvatierra — circular desde Torre de Miguel Sesmero&lt;/a&gt; is a popular moderate loop connecting two historic castles.

Q: What historical landmarks or natural monuments can I see along the trails?
A: The trails often lead to significant historical and natural sites. You can cycle past the impressive &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/6249042" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Nogales Castle&lt;/a&gt;, a 15th-century defensive fortress. Another notable natural monument is the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/1132207" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Ancient Olive Tree of La Tapada&lt;/a&gt;, an impressively large millennial olive tree. The &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/3045957" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Twelve Apostles Cork Oaks&lt;/a&gt; are also a unique natural feature you might encounter on routes like the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e1359926406/nogales-reservoir-and-12-apostles-loop" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Nogales Reservoir and 12 Apostles Loop&lt;/a&gt;.

Q: Are there any trails that pass by water features or reservoirs?
A: Yes, some routes incorporate water features. The &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e1359926406/nogales-reservoir-and-12-apostles-loop" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Nogales Reservoir and 12 Apostles Loop&lt;/a&gt; is a moderate trail that cycles around the Nogales Reservoir. Additionally, the region features other lagoons, fountains, and streams like the Hawk Creek (Arroyo del Halcón), adding to the scenic appeal.

Q: What is the best time of year for mountain biking in Torre de Miguel Sesmero?
A: The best time for mountain biking in Torre de Miguel Sesmero is generally during the spring and autumn months. The weather is milder, making for more comfortable riding conditions. Summers can be very hot, and winters can be cooler and wetter, though riding is possible year-round with appropriate preparation.

Q: Are the mountain bike trails in Torre de Miguel Sesmero dog-friendly?
A: While many natural areas in Extremadura are generally accessible, it's always recommended to check specific trail regulations regarding dogs, especially in protected areas or near wildlife habitats like Laguna Grande. If dogs are permitted, ensure they are well-behaved and kept on a leash to protect local wildlife and other trail users.

Q: What do other mountain bikers say about the trails in Torre de Miguel Sesmero?
A: The mountain bike trails in Torre de Miguel Sesmero are highly rated by the komoot community, with an average score of 5.0 stars. Reviewers often praise the diverse terrain, the blend of natural beauty with historical sites, and the well-maintained paths that offer a rewarding experience for various skill levels.

Q: Where can I find parking or trailheads for mountain biking in the area?
A: Torre de Miguel Sesmero serves as a common starting point for many routes. You can typically find parking within the town or at designated trailheads on the outskirts. Many routes, such as the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e1358790340/torre-de-miguel-sesmero-a-salvaleon-circular-por-el-castillo-de-nogales" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Torre de Miguel Sesmero a Salvaleón — circular por el Castillo de Nogales&lt;/a&gt;, begin directly from the town, making access convenient.

Q: Are there any options for food or accommodation near the mountain bike trails?
A: Torre de Miguel Sesmero, as a local town, offers basic amenities including cafes and pubs where you can refuel after your ride. For accommodation, you'll find options within the town or in nearby villages, ranging from guesthouses to rural lodgings, providing a convenient base for exploring the trails.
tips: Here you will find another quiet town in Extremadura where things move slowly and life is quite calm. Less than 2 kilometers from Almendral and a few more to Nogales, this is a good place to start an adventure or just make a stop during one.

Located in the Badajoz town of Nogales, this medieval fortress built in the 15th century sits on a small hill and its character is purely defensive. For this reason, its most outstanding element is the keep surrounded by a wall, since it was of great importance during the clashes with the French and the Portuguese.

Salvatierra de los Barros is a municipality known internationally for what has been one of its economic pillars for centuries: pottery. Along with the ceramic industry, the breeding of the Iberian pig raised in freedom among holm oaks and cork oaks stands out.

It is private property. You can go into the grounds but not inside the castle itself (which was renovated to be lived in).
Even if the gate is closed, you can pass, but you must close it again.
As a curiosity, it was bought and restored to live in by Vogue photographer Anthony Denney and his wife in 1972, with the man buried in the village cemetery. The wife, now very elderly, lives in Zafra.

faqs.question: Where can I park to access the mountain biking route?
faqs.answer: The circular route starts and ends in Torre de Miguel Sesmero. A good starting point is near the Plaza de la Constitución, Torre de Miguel Sesmero, where you should be able to find parking in the village.

faqs.question: Is this mountain biking trail suitable for beginners?
faqs.answer: This route is rated as 'moderate' and is best suited for riders with good physical fitness and some advanced experience. While some sections might be manageable for less experienced riders, the overall distance of 45.5 km and the 670 meters of elevation gain and loss suggest it's a challenging ride that requires a solid skill level.

faqs.question: What is the terrain like on the trail?
faqs.answer: The trail features a mix of beautiful and picturesque trails combined with singletracks that roll through the landscape. As a moderate mountain biking route, you can expect varied terrain, potentially including some technical sections, roots, or small obstacles, especially given its STS S2 classification.

faqs.question: What historical landmarks and natural features can I see along the route?
faqs.answer: You'll encounter several notable sights. The route passes by the impressive Nogales Castle, a 15th-century fortress offering extensive views. You'll also ride towards Salvatierra de los Barros and the Castillo de Salvatierra. The trail winds between the Monsalud and Rozallana mountains and is part of the 'route of the Twelve Apostles,' named for ancient cork oaks, seven of which still stand.

faqs.question: When is the best time of year to mountain bike this route?
faqs.answer: To enjoy the trail comfortably, the best times are typically spring and autumn when temperatures are milder. Summers in Extremadura can be very hot, making the 45.5 km distance more challenging. Winter can be pleasant, but be prepared for cooler temperatures and potentially wet conditions.

faqs.question: Is the trail dog-friendly?
faqs.answer: Generally, dogs are welcome on trails in this region. However, given the length and moderate difficulty of this mountain biking route, ensure your dog is fit enough for the distance and terrain. Always keep your dog under control, preferably on a leash, to protect wildlife and respect other trail users.

faqs.question: Do I need a permit or pay an entrance fee to ride this trail?
faqs.answer: Based on available information, there are no specific permits or entrance fees required to access this mountain biking trail. It's generally open to the public.

faqs.question: Are there any facilities like cafes or accommodation near the trailhead in Torre de Miguel Sesmero?
faqs.answer: Yes, Torre de Miguel Sesmero is a village, so you should find basic amenities such as cafes or small eateries where you can refuel before or after your ride. For accommodation, there might be local guesthouses or rural stays available in or around the village.

faqs.question: What should I bring for this mountain biking trip?
faqs.answer: For a moderate 45.5 km mountain bike ride, you should bring plenty of water, snacks, a repair kit for your bike (including a spare tube, pump, and basic tools), a first-aid kit, appropriate cycling attire, and sun protection. A fully charged phone with the Komoot app for navigation is also highly recommended.

faqs.question: Is there water available on the trail?
faqs.answer: There are no specific water sources mentioned directly on the trail. It's highly recommended to carry all the water you'll need for the entire 45.5 km route, especially on warmer days. You might find opportunities to refill in the villages you pass through, such as Salvatierra de los Barros.

faqs.question: What makes this route unique compared to other trails in Extremadura?
faqs.answer: This route stands out by connecting two significant historical castles, Nogales Castle and Castillo de Salvatierra, offering a blend of cultural heritage and natural beauty. Its inclusion as part of the 'route of the Twelve Apostles' with its ancient cork oaks also adds a unique historical and ecological dimension to the ride.

waypoints.name: Plaza de la Constitución, Torre de Miguel Sesmero
